title: Here’s Why Some Humans Can Hear Super Low-Frequency Sounds
gist: A Scientific Reports study probes how humans perceive infrasound below 20 Hz, waves technically outside the hearing range that emanate from furnaces and ventilation and that prior research links to raised cortisol and irritability.

insights.claim: Infrasound waves below 20 Hz, emitted by furnaces, generators, and ventilation systems, are technically outside the human hearing range yet there is substantial evidence people can perceive them.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Suggests an unrecognized environmental influence on human states.

insights.claim: Prior research shows infrasound played alongside music raises listeners' cortisol levels and makes them feel irritable, linking inaudible waves to feelings of unease.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: A physiological pathway from environmental infrasound to mood.
